#  Intel® Xeon® CPU Max Series 
## Get the only x86 processor with high-bandwidth memory.
[See product brief](https://www.intel.com/content/www/us/en/content-details/765366/intel-xeon-cpu-max-series-product-brief.html)
![Intel Xeon Max series logo with glowing background](https://intelcorp.scene7.com/is/image/intelcorp/intel-xeon-max-series-glow?qlt=80&wid=375&ts=1779203038604&&fmt=png8-alpha&dpr=on,1)
##  Intel Xeon CPU Max Series 
[ Highlights ](https://www.intel.com/content/www/us/en/products/details/processors/xeon/max-series.html#highlights)
[ Specifications ](https://www.intel.com/content/www/us/en/products/details/processors/xeon/max-series.html#specifications)
[ Videos ](https://www.intel.com/content/www/us/en/products/details/processors/xeon/max-series.html#videos)
[ Get started ](https://www.intel.com/content/www/us/en/products/details/processors/xeon/max-series.html#get-started)
[ Resources ](https://www.intel.com/content/www/us/en/products/details/processors/xeon/max-series.html#resources)
[ See product brief ](https://www.intel.com/content/www/us/en/content-details/765366/intel-xeon-cpu-max-series-product-brief.html)
* * *
##  Maximize bandwidth with the Intel Xeon CPU Max series 
Architected to supercharge the Intel® Xeon® platform with HBM, Intel Max series CPUs deliver up to 4.8x better performance compared to competition on real-world workloads1, such as modeling, AI, deep learning, high performance computing (HPC) and data analytics.
* * *
##  Get the power of up to 56 performance cores 
Built across four tiles and connected with Intel’s embedded multi-die interconnect bridge (EMIB) technology, all within a 350-watt envelope.
![Chip with multiple tiles and HBM memory](https://intelcorp.scene7.com/is/image/intelcorp/newsroom-intel-xeon-cpu-max-series-processor-glow:1080-1080?ts=1777585384215&&fmt=png8-alpha&dpr=on,1)
##  Tap into 64GB of high bandwidth memory 
Boost performance with 64GB of in-package HBM2e memory across four stacks, delivering ultra-high bandwidth and more than 1GB of memory per core.
![Stacked memory layers icon](https://intelcorp.scene7.com/is/image/intelcorp/memory-stack-illustration-xeon:1080-1080?ts=1777585350700&&fmt=png8-alpha&dpr=on,1)
##  Reduce costs and speed time to results 
Eliminate DDR and avoid complex code changes for workloads that fit within 64GB and need 1–2GB of memory per core.
![Engineer working on a laptop in a server room](https://intelcorp.scene7.com/is/image/intelcorp/a1137191-it-technician-laptop-in-server-room:1080-1080?ts=1777557505002&&fmt=png8-alpha&dpr=on,1)
  1. Get the power of up to 56 performance cores 
  2. Tap into 64GB of high bandwidth memory 
  3. Reduce costs and speed time to results 


Previous Next
* * *
##  Boost bandwidth for demanding workloads 
Intel Xeon CPU Max series processors are designed to accelerate the most demanding workloads, delivering next-level performance.
Up to
## 5
x
better performance for memory bandwidth vs. competition and previous generations.1 2
Up to
## 20
x
speed-up on Numenta AI technology for NLP with HBM compared to other CPUs.3
Up to 
## 8.6
x
performance gains with no code change over current HPC systems at Los Alamos National Laboratory.4
* * *

* * *
##  See testimonials and videos 
##  Ansys Fluent performance gains on Intel Max series CPUs 
See the more than 2X5 performance gains with Ansys Fluent on Intel Max series CPUs with HBM, a collaboration with Intel that is unlocking new levels of performance on compute-intensive HPC applications.
Watch 2 min
![Thermal simulation of a chip](https://intelcorp.scene7.com/is/image/intelcorp/ansys-fluent-performance-thumbnail:680-680?ts=1777557502913&&fmt=png8-alpha&dpr=on,1)
##  Intel + Ansys Better Together 
##  Introducing Intel Max series product family 
Get an inside look into the Intel Max series product family that delivers CPU with high bandwidth memory and up to 4.8x better performance compared to competition1 on real-world workloads.
Watch 2 min
![Gloved hand holding processor units](https://intelcorp.scene7.com/is/image/intelcorp/intel-max-series-intro-thumbnail:1080-1080?ts=1777557501026&&fmt=png8-alpha&dpr=on,1)
##  Intel® Max Series Family Introduction Video 
##  Fireside Chat: Intel Max series processors 
Get an inside look on how 4th Gen Intel® Xeon® processors and the Intel Max series product family, enabled through HPE, are advancing scientific innovation and discovery at Los Alamos National Lab.
Watch 3 min
![Man speaking in interview setting](https://intelcorp.scene7.com/is/image/intelcorp/intel-max-fireside-chat-thumbnail:1080-1080?ts=1777557499354&&fmt=png8-alpha&dpr=on,1)
##  Intel Max Series CPU Fireside Chat Video 
* * *
##  Discover product demos 
##  Acceleration with high bandwidth memory 
Discover the performance of a global atmosphere simulation MPAS-A workload running on Intel Xeon CPU Max series, which addresses memory bandwidth concerns of critical HPC applications, such as climate and weather models.
Watch 2 min
![Global climate simulation visualization](https://intelcorp.scene7.com/is/image/intelcorp/global-atmos-sim-thumbnail:680-680?ts=1777557495544&&fmt=png8-alpha&dpr=on,1)
##  Intel Max Series Product Demo: Acceleration with High Bandwidth Memory 
##  AlphaFold 2 on Intel Max series CPUs 
See the performance boost from Intel Max series CPUs with HBM, Intel® AMX and BF16 features on Deepmind’s AlphaFold 2 workload for inference.
Watch 4 sec
![Protein structure visualization](https://intelcorp.scene7.com/is/image/intelcorp/alphafold-thumbnail:680-680?ts=1778103927865&&fmt=png8-alpha&dpr=on,1)
##  AI Accelerated HPC on Alfphafold2 
##  HPC & AI performance on CosmoFlow 
View the performance boost of the MLPerf HPC AI benchmark CosmoFlow on the Intel Max series CPU, achieving up to 5x gains over competition and 2.7x over 3rd Gen Intel® Xeon® processors for deep learning training.1
Watch 4 sec
![Galaxy or space simulation visualization](https://intelcorp.scene7.com/is/image/intelcorp/cosmosflow-thumbnail:680-680?ts=1778103925726&&fmt=png8-alpha&dpr=on,1)
##  HPC-AI Performance on Cosmo Flow

* * *
## Product and Performance Information6
1 Visit [intel.com/performanceindex](https://edc.intel.com/content/www/us/en/products/performance/benchmarks/overview/) (Events: Supercomputing 22) for workloads and configurations. Results may vary.
  
2 2S Intel Xeon Max CPU vs. 2S AMD EPYC 7773X and 2S 3rd Gen Intel® Xeon® 8380
  
3 Numenta BERT-Large AMD Milan: Tested by Numenta as of 11/28/2022. 1-node, 2x AMD EPYC 7R13 on AWS m6a.48xlarge, 768 GB DDR4-3200, Ubuntu 20.04 Kernel 5.15, OpenVINO 2022.3, BERT-Large, Sequence Length 512, Batch Size 1. Intel® Xeon® 8480+: Tested by Numenta as of 11/28/2022. 1-node, 2x Intel® Xeon® 8480+, 512 GB DDR5-4800, Ubuntu 22.04 Kernel 5.17, OpenVINO 2022.3, Numenta-Optimized BERT-Large, Sequence Length 512, Batch Size 1. Intel® Xeon® Max 9468: Tested by Numenta as of 11/30/2022. 1-node, 2x Intel® Xeon® Max 9468, 128 GB HBM2e 3200 MT/s, Ubuntu 22.04 Kernel 5.15, OpenVINO 2022.3, Numenta-Optimized BERT-Large, Sequence Length 512, Batch Size 1.
  
4 On pre-production silicon on Crossroads. View <https://arxiv.org/abs/2211.05712> for workloads and configurations. Results may vary.
  
5 Performance varies by use, configuration, and other factors. Performance results are based on testing as of dates shown in configurations and may not reflect all publicly available ​updates. No product or component can be absolutely secure. Learn more at [www.Intel.com/PerformanceIndex](https://edc.intel.com/content/www/us/en/products/performance/benchmarks/overview/?r=1320097368 "Follow link")​.
  
6 Intel does not control or audit third-party data. You should consult other sources to evaluate accuracy.

    Intel® Xeon® CPU Max Series

    Get the only x86 processor with high-bandwidth memory.

    See product brief

    Intel Xeon CPU Max Series

    Highlights
    Specifications
    Videos
    Get started
    Resources
    See product brief

    Maximize bandwidth with the Intel Xeon CPU Max series 

    Architected to supercharge the Intel® Xeon® platform with HBM, Intel Max series CPUs deliver up to 4.8x better performance compared to competition on real-world workloads1, such as modeling, AI, deep learning, high performance computing (HPC) and data analytics.

    Get the power of up to 56 performance cores

    Built across four tiles and connected with Intel’s embedded multi-die interconnect bridge (EMIB) technology, all within a 350-watt envelope.

    Tap into 64GB of high bandwidth memory

    Boost performance with 64GB of in-package HBM2e memory across four stacks, delivering ultra-high bandwidth and more than 1GB of memory per core.

    Reduce costs and speed time to results

    Eliminate DDR and avoid complex code changes for workloads that fit within 64GB and need 1–2GB of memory per core.
    1. Get the power of up to 56 performance cores
    2. Tap into 64GB of high bandwidth memory
    3. Reduce costs and speed time to results

    Boost bandwidth for demanding workloads

    Intel Xeon CPU Max series processors are designed to accelerate the most demanding workloads, delivering next-level performance.

    Up to

    5

    x

    better performance for memory bandwidth vs. competition and previous generations.1 2

    Up to

    20

    x

    speed-up on Numenta AI technology for NLP with HBM compared to other CPUs.3

    Up to

    8.6

    x

    performance gains with no code change over current HPC systems at Los Alamos National Laboratory.4

    Product and Performance Information6

    1 Visit intel.com/performanceindex (Events: Supercomputing 22) for workloads and configurations. Results may vary.


    2 2S Intel Xeon Max CPU vs. 2S AMD EPYC 7773X and 2S 3rd Gen Intel® Xeon® 8380


    3 Numenta BERT-Large AMD Milan: Tested by Numenta as of 11/28/2022. 1-node, 2x AMD EPYC 7R13 on AWS m6a.48xlarge, 768 GB DDR4-3200, Ubuntu 20.04 Kernel 5.15, OpenVINO 2022.3, BERT-Large, Sequence Length 512, Batch Size 1. Intel® Xeon® 8480+: Tested by Numenta as of 11/28/2022. 1-node, 2x Intel® Xeon® 8480+, 512 GB DDR5-4800, Ubuntu 22.04 Kernel 5.17, OpenVINO 2022.3, Numenta-Optimized BERT-Large, Sequence Length 512, Batch Size 1. Intel® Xeon® Max 9468: Tested by Numenta as of 11/30/2022. 1-node, 2x Intel® Xeon® Max 9468, 128 GB HBM2e 3200 MT/s, Ubuntu 22.04 Kernel 5.15, OpenVINO 2022.3, Numenta-Optimized BERT-Large, Sequence Length 512, Batch Size 1.


    4 On pre-production silicon on Crossroads. View https://arxiv.org/abs/2211.05712 for workloads and configurations. Results may vary.


    5 Performance varies by use, configuration, and other factors. Performance results are based on testing as of dates shown in configurations and may not reflect all publicly available ​updates. No product or component can be absolutely secure. Learn more at www.Intel.com/PerformanceIndex​.


    6 Intel does not control or audit third-party data. You should consult other sources to evaluate accuracy.
